paragraphs.source_preview: Power prices misconception, PJM's poor market design, Capacity prices 9.3x growth, ERCOT vs PJM grid reliability and expansion
By Aishwarya Mahesh, Jeremie Eliahou Ontiveros, Ajey Pandey, and 2 others
Mar 03, 2026 · Paid

sections.title: Are AI Datacenters Increasing Electric Bills for American Households?
sections.title_translation: AI数据中心推高了美国家庭的电费吗？

paragraphs.source_preview: SemiAnalysis x Fluidstack is launching GTC with a 48-hour, full-stack AI infrastructure hackathon on March 15th from Power to Prefill, Dirt to Decode. With spea
The topic of datacenter load growth and impact on power prices remains broadly misunderstood, akin to the water consumption myth that we debunked recently. It w
This report explores the question by analyzing the two biggest energy markets in the US, which are also the largest AI Datacenter hubs: the PJM interconnection
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!YaBK!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: SemiAnalysis Energy Model, PJM, Monitoring Analytics
In PJM, we think poor market design is the main culprit. Most of the 15% increase in household electricity bills in PJM is driven by a widely misunderstood and
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!4uxO!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: PJM BRA Report
Many are finger-pointing at the surge in AI datacenters, and it is understandable. The PJM area is at the forefront of the AI boom, with Google notably training
Now look at Texas. The state is witnessing an equivalent AI buildout, with OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Anthropic all building massive facilities. Yet power futures
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!bTKf!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: Bloomberg
Let’s dig in. This report focuses on ERCOT and PJM since they’re the two largest energy markets in the country, and are epicenters of the AI revolution. We’ll d
Then, behind paywall, we discuss the supply chain implications. We believe that market constraints are dramatically shifting and many are missing it. This shift
For institutions looking for deeper analysis, subscribe to our Energy Model and Datacenter Industry Model. The latter tracks and forecasts quarter-by-quarter, 2
Let’s start with a brief explainer of what “capacity” actually is, how it flows to your monthly bill, and then dig further into market designs.
This report is a collaboration between SemiAnalysis and Archer Daniels Midland Investor Services (ADMIS), a leading futures brokerage and clearing firm.

sections.title: Capacity: paying for power plants to sit idle 95% of the time
sections.title_translation: 容量：为95%时间闲置的发电厂买单

paragraphs.source_preview: At a high level, a monthly household electric bill is driven by the following charges:
Energy: In deregulated territories (including PJM’s 13 states, and ERCOT/Texas), this is a function of wholesale prices, i.e. real-time supply and demand for el
Capacity: This charge doesn’t exist in ERCOT but matters in PJM. It is a function of supply & demand for capacity, i.e. power that will only turn on a few hours
Transmission & Distribution (T&D): the charges for the electrical distribution network.
This remains a highly regulated area. T&D providers typically earn a pre-determined, regulated Return on Equity. As such, utilization rate of transmission & dis
Others: taxes, retail adders, ancillary services, etc. We will not dive into this, as it varies based on location.
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Y-ZQ!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: SemiAnalysis estimates, EIA
The purpose of capacity markets is to ensure households and businesses always have the electricity they need, including on peak summer or winter days. For conte
In PJM, to ensure we have capacity when it’s needed, we pay power plant owners to maintain their assets on standby for >95% of the year. The price paid is deter
ERCOT, on the other hand, is an “energy-only” market, with no separate capacity auction. Real-time pricing signals determine “scarcity” and incentivizes power p
The core issue with the capacity market design is that it is directly impacted by the supply & demand forecast of a central planner, PJM. Any forecasting error
How the Base Residual Auction Works
PJM pays for system capacity with the aforementioned forward capacity market, called the Base Residual Auction (BRA). This is a yearly auction ran two years ahe
Unlike wholesale energy markets that trade in $/MWh (i.e. electrons consumed in a given hour), the BRA trades in $/MW-day (i.e. peak power provisioned in a give
Until recently, the BRA delivered on its promise. Summer 2025 was brutally hot in PJM, with June 23 and 24 setting records as the 3rd- and 4th-highest peak days
But that reliability now comes at an extraordinary price. Between June 2024 and May 2025 (the 2024/25 service period), capacity cost $29/MW-day. For the current
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!48ZG!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: PJM BRA Report
PJM blamed “runaway” power costs on both extreme weather and hyperscaler datacenter & AI power demand, and that narrative trickled into mainstream news. But tha
The Simulation Under the Hood
Capacity prices are based on an artificial supply-demand curve known internally as the Variable Resource Requirement (VRR) curve. The VRR curve is built on PJM’
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!H6mE!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
However, the VRR curve is constructed from a web of assumptions, many of which depend on non-public models and proprietary data. Even modest changes in forecast
Datacenters blamed as the culprit for surging capacity prices
PJM’s Internal Market Monitor (IMM) -- an independent monitoring entity required by the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission (FERC) -- ran alternate simulations
Removing all datacenters from the forecast reduced PJM peak load by 7,927 MW, resulting in a $9.33 billion reduction of total capacity payments -- a 64% reducti
Keeping only already-energized datacenters reduced peak load by 4,654 MW, resulting in a $7.74 billion reduction -- a 53% cut from the actual price. For the 202
According to the IMM’s analysis, incremental datacenter load growth alone explains roughly a doubling in capacity costs versus a hypothetical grid without that
But all of these simulations obscure a deeper issue: the main auction that drives electric rates is also based on a simulation. The VRR curve is an artificial s
And we believe the forecast is inaccurate. Our methodology tracks precise construction timelines of every single datacenter in the PJM area and shows PJM’s fore
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!prZB!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: SemiAnalysis Datacenter Model, PJM
We show below a great example of this. PJM’s own data shows an inability to forecast even one year out. In 2024, the datacenter load forecast was cut by 800MW v
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!U3MK!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: PJM, Monitoring Analytics
Forward Energy Prices Tell a Different Story
The energy market in PJM remains closer to a real market, with supply and demand balances that return a moving price per MWh of electricity. These prices spike
PJM Western Hub forward prices -- the most liquid benchmark for energy traders’ view of the future -- have increased 12-20% in the 2028 and 2030 windows, with t
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!aZkY!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: Bloomberg
PJM’s supply-side forecast is also simulated
PJM’s forecast and methodology are also impacting the supply side of the forecast. A year before the AI Datacenter boom, issues had already begun to rise. As sh
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!0RvI!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: PJM
As shown below, while coal retirements were the biggest drivers, PJM also introduced major methodology changes that caused close to 20GW of supply to disappear.
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!lio-!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: PJM
How Capacity Prices Hit Household Bills
The 2026/27 Base Residual Auction (BRA) clearing price of $329/MW-day represents a tangible cost increase for every load in PJM. These costs are ultimately reco
To estimate the impact on retail bills, we need the following datapoints:
· Average power consumption per household: in PJM, that’s 880 kWh per month.
· “Load factor”, i.e. average usage to peak usage. Empirical data shows 40% as a common value.
· Capacity prices: at $329/MW-day, we can divide this by the number of hours per day, and apply the 0.4 load factor, to get $34/MWh (or 3.4c/kWh).
Multiplying 3.4 by the monthly consumption (880 kWh) gets us a $29.9 monthly payment. Given the auction has already been cleared, we have near-certainty that ho
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!YaBK!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: SemiAnalysis Energy Model, PJM, Monitoring Analytics
Let’s now turn our attention to Texas, to see how power prices have responded to the massive AI Datacenter surge.

sections.title: ERCOT: Same Load Growth, No Price Shock
sections.title_translation: ERCOT：同等负载增长，无价格冲击

paragraphs.source_preview: The Electric Reliability Council of Texas (ERCOT) is a much simpler market to understand. It runs a unified market mechanism balancing supply and demand based o
Scarcity Pricing Instead of a Capacity Auction
Instead of a capacity market driven by a once-a-year auction, ERCOT uses a real-time scarcity price adder based on an Operating Reserve Demand Curve (ORDC). Whe
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!pKKs!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
This scarcity-price structure allows capacity resources that run fewer than 100 hours per year (gas peaker plants, batteries, etc) to pay for themselves, becaus
Said differently: in PJM, the central operator has the responsibility to analyze the system, determine capacity needs, and guarantees payment to power plant own
ERCOT’s Demand Forecast: Extraordinary -- and Largely Ignored
This difference is particularly interesting as ERCOT also provides demand forecasts. And the outlook is staggering. The 2025 Long-Term Load Forecast, released A
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!Dqhd!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: ERCOT
Taken at face value, that forecast implies a structural demand shock on the order of an entire new ERCOT system layered on top of today’s load curve. In reality
Even ERCOT recognized that their forecast was not realized and changed course. In the May 2025 Capacity, Demand and Reserves report, they applied a deliberate h
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!sY9J!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: ERCOT
But the difference here is that ERCOT’s load forecasts and interconnection queue constraints do not directly drive electricity prices. PJM’s forecasts drive a s
More Datacenter Load, Less Scarcity
The physical system confirms ERCOT’s approach is working. The grid has already experienced record-breaking peaks of over 90 GW in summer 2024 and a spring recor
The prices that energy traders see have not gone to the moon either. Forward prices -- particularly 2026, 2028, and 2030 contracts -- have increased 11-17% in t
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!bTKf!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: Bloomberg
The 2024 Biennial ORDC Report explains that more online reserves were available than in prior cycles, allowing for steady, calm growth. Solar, wind, batteries,
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!bbjn!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: ERCOT, SemiAnalysis annotation
The forward wholesale price curve tells us that traders believe ERCOT can absorb the growth. They are betting on supply expansion, improved reserves, and SB 6 c
ERCOT also moves much faster: one of its key advantages over PJM is that it only covers one state and is not subject to FERC jurisdiction. On the other hand, PJ

sections.title: Winter Storm Fern: Paying for Reliability vs. Delivering It
sections.title_translation: 冬季风暴“弗恩”：为可靠性买单 vs 实际交付

paragraphs.source_preview: Winter Storm Fern (January 24-27, 2026) was the first real-world stress test of PJM’s record-high 2025/26 capacity prices and of ERCOT’s market discipline under
ERCOT: No Crisis
ERCOT’s grid held through the January freeze. The Weather Watch issued by the grid operator remained precautionary. Demand ran below forecasts, no emergency pro
This was the proof that Texas had learned its lesson from the disastrous Winter Storm Uri. The post-Uri reforms -- mandatory winterization of gas production and
Real-time prices in ERCOT peaked around $300/MWh.
PJM: $270/MW-Day Bought a 21 GW Failure
PJM’s grid fared dramatically worse. Despite capacity markets having already priced in datacenter load risk through record-high clearing prices, the grid lost a
The Department of Energy was forced to issue emergency orders under Section 202(c) of the Federal Power Act, authorizing grid operators to bypass environmental
Real-time electricity prices reflected the operational stress. PJM averaged $700/MWh system-wide, with Virginia’s datacenter-heavy Dominion zone spiking to $1,8
That also demonstrates another failure of PJM’s capacity market. In PJM, plants get paid no matter what, even if they fail when needed. In ERCOT, power plants o
What the Storm Revealed
The storm exposed the fundamental disconnect in PJM’s capacity market. High capacity prices had been driven by forecasts of datacenter load growth -- a risk tha
ERCOT had not priced speculative datacenter growth into elevated capacity charges, yet its mandatory operational reforms delivered grid stability when tested. L
The 35 GW of datacenter backup generation identified by DOE during the storm also demonstrated something important: datacenters can function as grid resources w
The Political Fallout Is Already Here
PJM’s simulation-driven pricing has made the capacity market a political target. After the 2025/26 auction spike, Pennsylvania Governor Josh Shapiro filed a FER
But the cap didn’t fix the underlying mechanism. The VRR curve, the demand forecast methodology, all remain unchanged. The bandaid also created a new problem: a
Attempts have been made to change the PJM’s structures, but regulations and considerations are fierce. PJM’s attempt to introduce Non-Capacity-Backed Load (NCBL
The Regulatory Asymmetry
ERCOT can move faster because its regulatory structure is simpler. ERCOT’s service territory is entirely within Texas, so the Texas legislature and Public Utili
This structural asymmetry is durable and investable. ERCOT will continue to be able to adapt its market rules and operational requirements faster than PJM can r

Since mid-2025, our Datacenter Industry Model has shown a huge flip in market constraints. While energy used to be the core bottleneck, we are now seeing more “
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!p39e!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: SemiAnalysis Datacenter Industry Model
The rise of onsite gas has dramatically alleviated the energy constraints. Over a month ago, we explained that the market is already seeing 12 different equipme
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!oMuQ!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: ERCOT
The constraint is also shifting from energy to construction. Data from ERCOT shows that “approved to energize” campuses are not even using their full draw – typ
All of this means that many utility forecasts in 2027 and beyond are overstated. Even if utilities claim their forecasts are backed by ESAs and LOAs and backed
As our recent institutional-only article “Nvidia as the Central Bank of AI” called out, hyperscalers are using power to squeeze out other players from the marke
Lastly, in 2027, we see the overall constraint shifting from power to silicon. The industry cannot procure enough memory and logic and more shortages are ahead.
As such, we think energy constraints are now alleviating, which means downside risk for IPPs, overexposed to core grid constraints.
https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!OgV8!,w_1456,c_limit,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic
Source: SemiAnalysis Memory Model
For cryptominers and datacenter developers, this means more competition. Grid power remains premium, but the delivery timeline is often unreliable. For example,
